constraints: Must use an index (dict) on the right list.
Output must preserve left record order.
disallowed_assumptions: Each left key matches at most one right key.
The right list is always small enough for nested loops.
expected_failure_modes: Not including all right matches for a left record.
prohibited_claims: Model superiority — not authorized by this task.
Wrapper improvement without paired comparison — not measured.
prompt:
Implement `join(left: list[dict], right: list[dict], left_key: str, right_key: str) -> list[dict]`.

Perform an inner join on two lists of dicts.
For each record in `left`, find all matching records in `right` where `record[left_key] == right_record[right_key]`.
Return a list of merged dicts (all keys from left + all keys from right, prefixed with 'right_' to avoid collisions).

Constraint: Must use a dict-based lookup index (not nested loops).
Complexity must be O(n + m) average, not O(n × m).

Handle: left or right being empty, records with missing join key (skip that record), multiple matches in right for a single left record (include all combinations).
public_contract: join(left, right, left_key, right_key) -> list[dict] with O(n+m) average complexity
title: Use Indexing/Map-Based Lookup for Join
